content:
Case 9:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 81
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: New-onset swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm with localized pain and reduced range of motion
- Vital signs: BP 118/70 mmHg, HR 92/min (irregular), Temp 36.9 °C, RR 18/min, SpO₂ 95 % (room air)
Setting: Outpatient clinic (initial presentation with localized musculoskeletal swelling)
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient in an ambulatory hematology setting. You can obtain a detailed history and examination, request routine blood work, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging appropriate for an initial assessment. Invasive biopsy procedures and complex diagnostic evaluations require additional planning.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "physical examination"}}

History Physical:
2025-07-03: The patient presented to the hematology outpatient clinic with new-onset swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm, which developed over the past several weeks and was associated with localized pain and reduced range of motion. He also reported foamy urine, but denied constitutional B symptoms such as fever, night sweats, or unintentional weight loss. There was no recent history of infection or trauma. Physical examination revealed asymmetric swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm with mild tenderness to palpation. No overlying erythema or warmth was noted. No palpable cervical, axillary, or inguinal lymphadenopathy. Lungs were clear on auscultation; cardiac rhythm was irregularly irregular. The abdomen was soft and non-tender without hepatosplenomegaly. No peripheral edema was present. Neurological exam showed intact cranial nerves and normal gait.
Current medication: allopurinol 100 mg 1-0-0, calcium carbonate 500 mg 1-0-0, cotrimoxazole 480 mg 1-0-0 (Mon/Wed/Fri), tamsulosin 0.4 mg 0-0-1, ramipril 5 mg 1-0-0, torasemide 10 mg 0-1-0, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-0, ophthalmic timolol 0.5% (1 drop both eyes BID), atorvastatin 10 mg 0-0-1, apixaban 2.5 mg 1-0-1, bisoprolol 2.5 mg 1-0-1, and pregabalin 75 mg 1-0-1.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "right shoulder imaging (X-ray) with AP and axillary views"}}

response.request: right shoulder imaging (X-ray) with AP and axillary views

content:
'right shoulder imaging (X-ray) with AP and axillary views' not available. Try 'ct_skeleton' instead.
This case does not list plain radiography of the shoulder. Skeletal imaging is available as CT skeleton findings — you can request the CT/skeletal imaging report if that would help.
(4 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "CT skeleton imaging report"}}

Ct Skeleton:
2025-07-03: CT skeleton showed multiple osteolytic lesions of the axial and appendicular skeleton, most prominent in vertebrae, ribs, and pelvis, without signs of new destruction, consistent with inactive post-treatment myeloma lesions. Soft-tissue swelling at the right shoulder and proximal arm incompletely characterized due to lack of contrast.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "serum protein electrophoresis with immunofixation"}}

'protein_studies' available in round 2.
This case includes serum and urine protein electrophoresis and immunofixation, but those protein studies are not available in round 1. Consider requesting labs or imaging available this round (e.g., chemistry_immunology, CBC, or CT_skeleton).
(4 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "MRI of the right shoulder with contrast"}}

response.request: MRI of the right shoulder with contrast

content:
'MRI of the right shoulder with contrast' not available. Try 'ct_skeleton' instead.
This case doesn't list an MRI of the shoulder, but skeletal imaging is included. You could request the CT skeleton report to get available cross-sectional skeletal imaging findings.
(3 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "complete blood count"}}

Cbc Coagulation:
CBC: Hb 10.4 g/dL, WBC 6.1 × 10⁹/L, Plt 104 × 10⁹/L; MCV 90 fL, MCH 29 pg. Differential: neutrophils 60 %, lymphocytes 28 %, monocytes 8 %, eosinophils 3 %, basophils 1 %.
Coagulation: aPTT 31 s, INR 1.6.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "serum protein electrophoresis with immunofixation"}}

'serum protein electrophoresis with immunofixation' not available. Request a specific test or report.
The case includes serum and urine protein electrophoresis and immunofixation, but those results are not available in this round. Consider requesting available labs (e.g., chemistry_immunology or CBC/coagulation) or other imaging/exam items for information you can get now.
(4 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "serum free light chain assay (kappa and lambda with ratio)"}}

response.request: serum free light chain assay (kappa and lambda with ratio)

content:
'protein_studies' available in round 2.
Serum/urine myeloma studies (including SPEP/UPEP, immunofixation, and free light chains) are listed under protein_studies but are scheduled for a later round. In this round you can request available labs such as CBC or chemistry_immunology instead.
(3 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "chemistry_immunology"}}

Chemistry Immunology:
Chemistry: Na 134 mmol/L, K 5.1 mmol/L, creatinine 2.3 mg/dL (eGFR 26 mL/min/1.73 m²), BUN 48 mg/dL, AST 28 U/L, ALT 32 U/L, bilirubin 0.9 mg/dL, LDH 265 U/L, uric acid 8.2 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 3.02 mmol/L, phosphate 4.9 mg/dL.
Immunology: IgG 1820 mg/dL, free κ light chains 412 mg/L, κ/λ ratio 48.
Urinalysis: protein +++ without hematuria.
NT-proBNP 2350 pg/mL.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bone marrow biopsy result (plasma cell percentage)"}}

response.request: bone marrow biopsy result (plasma cell percentage)

content:
'bone_marrow' available in round 2.
Bone marrow findings (including plasma-cell percentage) are in a later round. Consider requesting items available now such as CBC/coagulation, chemistry/immunology, or CT skeleton to proceed.
(4 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Active multiple myeloma with CRAB features (hypercalcemia, renal impairment, anemia, and osteolytic bone lesions) in an elderly male with a monoclonal IgG kappa clone (elevated free kappa light chains and abnormal kappa/lambda ratio).", "differentials": ["Active multiple myeloma with end-organ damage", "Metastatic bone disease from a solid tumor with secondary hypercalcemia", "Lymphoplasmacytic lymphoma or other plasma cell dyscrasia with bone involvement"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Initiate aggressive intravenous isotonic saline hydration for hypercalcemia and renal perfusion", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Administer calcitonin for rapid reduction of serum calcium while definitive therapy is initiated", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Start denosumab 120 mg subcutaneously every 4 weeks (with calcium and vitamin D supplementation) to protect bone and aid hypercalcemia management given renal impairment", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiate a bortezomib-based systemic anti-myeloma regimen tailored for frail elderly with renal impairment (e.g., bortezomib-containing triplet such as VCd)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Obtain diagnostic confirmation with bone marrow biopsy to assess plasma cell percentage and clonality", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Monitor and optimize supportive care: CBC, CMP including calcium and creatinine, repeat heavy-light chain studies when available, transfuse red cells if indicated, and manage infection risk", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Active multiple myeloma with CRAB features (hypercalcemia, renal impairment, anemia, and osteolytic bone lesions) in an elderly male with a monoclonal IgG kappa clone (elevated free kappa light chains and abnormal kappa/lambda ratio).
differentials: Active multiple myeloma with end-organ damage
Metastatic bone disease from a solid tumor with secondary hypercalcemia
Lymphoplasmacytic lymphoma or other plasma cell dyscrasia with bone involvement

treatment_plan.action: Initiate aggressive intravenous isotonic saline hydration for hypercalcemia and renal perfusion
Administer calcitonin for rapid reduction of serum calcium while definitive therapy is initiated
Start denosumab 120 mg subcutaneously every 4 weeks (with calcium and vitamin D supplementation) to protect bone and aid hypercalcemia management given renal impairment
Initiate a bortezomib-based systemic anti-myeloma regimen tailored for frail elderly with renal impairment (e.g., bortezomib-containing triplet such as VCd)
Obtain diagnostic confirmation with bone marrow biopsy to assess plasma cell percentage and clonality
Monitor and optimize supportive care: CBC, CMP including calcium and creatinine, repeat heavy-light chain studies when available, transfuse red cells if indicated, and manage infection risk

Interdisciplinary consultations are available. Some specialized test results may still be pending.
Request additional information or solve.

solve.working_diagnosis: Suspected active multiple myeloma with CRAB features (anemia, hypercalcemia, renal impairment, osteolytic bone lesions) in an elderly man with IgG kappa monoclonal protein.
differentials: Active multiple myeloma with end-organ damage
Metastatic bone disease from a solid tumor
Lymphoplasmacytic lymphoma or other plasma cell dyscrasia with bone involvement

treatment_plan.action: Bone marrow biopsy with flow cytometry and FISH cytogenetics
Comprehensive staging imaging with PET-CT or whole-body MRI
Initiate aggressive IV hydration with isotonic saline and begin calcium management
Calcitonin for rapid correction of hypercalcemia
Initiate denosumab 120 mg subcutaneously every 4 weeks with calcium and vitamin D supplementation
Initiate frail-elderly myeloma therapy using a renal-friendly triplet (e.g., daratumumab-based regimen with bortezomib and dexamethasone) with dose adjustments
Nephrology and supportive care consultations; monitor CBC, CMP, urinalysis; transfuse red cells or manage infections as indicated

Setting: Specialist hematology conference (definitive diagnosis and treatment planning)
Capabilities: You now have access to definitive pathology, cytogenetics, molecular findings, full staging, and a detailed treatment history. You are expected to integrate these data with comorbidity and transplant scores to establish the final diagnosis, risk stratification, and a realistic treatment and follow-up strategy.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

monitor calcium closely", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Nephrology and supportive care consultations; optimize renal protection, monitor CBC/CMP, treat anemia with transfusion if indicated, prevent/treat infections, and manage uric acid", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Assess transplant eligibility; if the patient is medically fit, plan for autologous stem cell transplantation after induction; if not transplant-eligible, continue with a fixed induction course (e.g., 4\u20136 cycles) followed by appropriate maintenance", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Ongoing response monitoring and supportive care: serial labs including calcium, creatinine, Hb, and albumin; solve.working_diagnosis: Active multiple myeloma with CRAB features in an elderly man (anemia: Hb 10.4 g/dL; hypercalcemia: Ca 3.02 mmol/L; renal impairment: creatinine 2.3 mg/dL, eGFR ~26; osteolytic bone lesions on CT skeleton; IgG kappa monoclonal protein with elevated free kappa light chains and abnormal kappa/lambda ratio).
differentials: Active multiple myeloma with end-organ damage
Metastatic bone disease from a solid tumor
Lymphoplasmacytic lymphoma or other plasma cell dyscrasia with bone involvement

treatment_plan.action: Bone marrow biopsy with flow cytometry and FISH cytogenetics
Comprehensive staging imaging with PET-CT or whole-body MRI
Initiate renal-friendly induction therapy for frail elderly multiple myeloma with hypercalcemia: daratumumab, bortezomib, and dexamethasone (D-Vd) with dose adjustments as needed

Continue and optimize hypercalcemia management: intravenous isotonic saline hydration, calcitonin for rapid correction if needed, and denosumab 120 mg subcutaneously every 4 weeks with calcium/vitamin D supplementation; monitor calcium closely
Nephrology and supportive care consultations; optimize renal protection, monitor CBC/CMP, treat anemia with transfusion if indicated, prevent/treat infections, and manage uric acid

Assess transplant eligibility; if the patient is medically fit, plan for autologous stem cell transplantation after induction; if not transplant-eligible, continue with a fixed induction course (e.g., 4–6 cycles) followed by appropriate maintenance
Ongoing response monitoring and supportive care: serial labs including calcium, creatinine, Hb, and albumin; serum/urine M-protein or free light chains as available; imaging as indicated
